title: lithocarpus koordersii
intro: Lithocarpus koordersii, also known as the Burmese tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Lithocarpus koordersii include reducing fever, alleviating cough, and treating respiratory infections, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, Lithocarpus koordersii is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and extracts that can be consumed as teas, tonics, or added to skincare products for its purported benefits.
